title: Tune JVM garbage collection from GC logs, not guesses
description: Capture GC logs and heap behavior, identify whether pauses or allocation rate is the problem, and adjust collector or heap settings with evidence.
body: Steps: 1. Enable unified GC logging (-Xlog:gc*:file=gc.log:time) and reproduce the load. 2. Analyze pause times, frequency, and allocation rate (a tool like GCViewer/JDK Mission Control). 3. Diagnose: frequent full GCs suggest a too-small heap or a leak; long pauses suggest trying G1 vs ZGC/Shenandoah for low latency. 4. Change one setting (heap size, collector, region/pause target) and re-measure. 5. Compare pause percentiles before/after. Rollback point: before raising -Xmx near physical RAM (swapping is worse than GC). Validation: GC logs show reduced pause percentiles and stable old-gen occupancy with no rising leak trend. Risk boundary: tuning flags cannot fix a memory leak; if old-gen keeps climbing after full GC, escalate to a heap-dump leak analysis instead of enlarging the heap.
